answer:
I think people can absolutely be happy without spending a lot of money. You know, happiness often comes from experiences and connections rather than material things. For example, I remember last summer when my friends and I decided to have a picnic at the local park. We didn't spend much at all—just some snacks we made at home and a blanket. It was one of the best days I've had; we played games, talked about our dreams, and just enjoyed each other's company. 

Also, I've found that engaging in hobbies like hiking or painting doesn't require much money but provides immense joy. Just being outside in nature or exploring my creativity makes me feel fulfilled and happy. I mean, it's those moments that really stick with you, not the latest gadget or trendy outfit.

But, on the flip side, it's easy to get caught up in the idea that happiness is linked to spending money. Social media sometimes makes it seem like we need to buy certain things to be happy. However, I believe true contentment comes from appreciating simple pleasures. 

In conclusion, while money can enhance some experiences, I think the core of happiness lies in relationships, experiences, and personal growth. So, yeah, I'd say that people can definitely find happiness without spending a ton of money—it's all about perspective!
title: Can people be happy without spending money? What do you think about this idea? Give examples from your life to explain your view.
